body_txt: A step down from the Black Shirts and Les Collaborateurs , but a step up from Apathetic Citizens , Gullible Lemmings are ordinary folks who don't just fail to recognise the Villain with Good Publicity as evil but actively help and support them — no threats or brainwashing required, these people are Super Gullible and easily fooled. If the villain is persuasive enough, everyone in the village, town, city, country, or any other kind of community will back them , quickly resulting in a Hero with Bad Publicity . Innocent Bystanders will be convinced that snitching on the hero(es), disrupting their plans and backing up the Legions of Terror are the right things to do , often making life considerably more difficult for our protagonists — for one, they won't want to hurt these people , no matter how deluded they may be, and even if they have no qualms or choice about this, it only serves to confirm that they're a dangerous menace . The trope can both be Played for Drama (if the villain is a genuinely cunning manipulator who has used plausible strategies to get the populace on side), or Played for Laughs (when the villain is an Obviously Evil cackling maniac but people still think that he seems like a swell guy ). Gullible Lemmings generally facilitate the existence of the Easily Conquered World and are particularly susceptible to the Propaganda Machine . Compare and contrast Civilians Are Irrelevant , where the general population may as well not exist. This trope is named after, but not to be confused with, Suicidal Lemmings , the hoax about actual lemmings jumping off cliffs to their doom. While some examples of this trope will ( directly or indirectly ) draw on historical examples of populations falling under the sway of demagogues, No Real Life Examples, Please! , as this trope involves people being easily manipulated by villains. noreallife
